prompt: A paper claims that explicit multi-agent orchestration improves multi-hop reasoning. Propose a simple experiment that can distinguish explicit coordination gains from gains that come only from extra inference compute.
objectives: Separate coordination effects from compute effects

prompt: Write a concise argument for why role specialization through prompts may emulate some benefits of a true multi-agent system even when all agents share the same underlying weights.
context: Assume the audience is a technical reviewer for an LLM systems paper.
constraints: Do not claim it is identical to a true multi-agent system
